ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-08-2009 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFFS ROY AND FLORENCE GUTHERY'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE/PREFERENTIAL SETTING, GRANTED, PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A) & (D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 3/22/2010 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 FOR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE DATES AND TO GIVE NOTICE TO ALL PARTIES. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL TRIAL DATE. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. ALL AND ANY MOTIONS, EXCEPT MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATIONS, ARE TO BE HEARD IN DEPT. 206. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 1/7/2010. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 1/27/2010. IF DEFENDANTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: E. BURA; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Apportionment Proceeding And For An Order To Distribute Settlement Funds
Off Calendar Mar-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Dept. 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6848

Amir Sarreshtehdary, Esq., appearing for Plaintiffs.
Plaintiffs' motion for an Order to Distribute the Funds in this action is heard this day.
The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the documentary evidence, and good cause appearing, IT IS ORDERED THAT:
1. The following are entitled to distribution of all net funds derived from the personal injury - survival and wrongful death causes of action resulting from settlements negotiated after the July 21, 2012 death of KATY JENNINGS, in the following proportions:
ESTATE OF ROY GUTHERY
(Asbestos-injured Party)
for Personal Injury - Survival 20%
ESTATE OF FLORENCE GUTHERY
(Surviving Spose)
as Wrongful Death Heir 50%
ESTATE OF KATHY JENNINGS
(Adult Child)
as Wrongful Death Heir 10%
LISA FENWICK
(Adult Grandchild)
as Wrongful Death Heir 10%
TAWNY KRAMP
Adult Grandchild)
as Wrongful Death Heir 10%
---------------------------------------------------
100%
Brayton & Purcell LLP shall recover from the net settlement funds of $15,974.34 currenty in Brayton & Purcell LLP's possession the sum of $3,056.80 as reasonable attorneys' fees and costs incurred in this proceeding, leaving the resulting net amount of $12,917.54.
The balance of the funds remaining after deduction of reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred in this proceeding, subject first to any liens, including any liens payable to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services' Medicare program and the California Department of Healt Care Services Medi-Cal Program, and subject to any holds placed on funds awaiting response from such agencies, shall be delivered by Brayton & Purcess LLP to the above persons, in the percentages set forth in paragraph 1 hereinabove.
Any net funds recovered in the future that are derived from settlements made after the July 21, 2012 death of KATHY JENNINGS, after costs and attorneys fees, shall be distributed in the perecentages set forth in paragraph 1 hereinabove.
The signature of Lisa Fenwick shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate claim and the wrongful death claims with defendants in the filed asbestos personal injury - survival and wrongful death action and in those claims made against bankruptcy-related trusts establiched for asbestos victims.

Court singed Orde in open court.
